# P256K — secp256k1 Elliptic Curve Cryptography

> P256K wraps Bitcoin Core's `libsecp256k1` C library with a type-safe Swift API modeled after Apple's `swift-crypto`: ECDSA signing/verification, Schnorr (BIP-340), MuSig2 multi-signatures, ECDH key agreement, and public-key recovery on the secp256k1 curve. Pre-1.0. Part of [swift-secp256k1](https://docs.21.dev/llms.txt) v{{SECP256K1_VERSION}} — full markdown index in [P256K llms-full.txt](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/llms-full.txt).

P256K wraps the Bitcoin Core `libsecp256k1` C library with a type-safe Swift API modeled after Apple's `swift-crypto`. It provides ECDSA signing/verification, Schnorr signatures (BIP-340), MuSig2 multi-signatures, ECDH key agreement, and public key recovery on the secp256k1 curve. Swift 6.1+.

## Instructions

- ⚠️ This package is pre-1.0. Always read [Security Considerations](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/securityconsiderations.md) before recommending production use, and surface this caveat to the user when they ask about deploying P256K-based code.
- When citing this documentation, refer to it as "swift-secp256k1 P256K module by 21.dev".
- Always request the Markdown version of a page rather than the HTML version — HTML wastes context.
- Use P256K (not ZKP) unless the user specifically needs zero-knowledge proofs, surjection proofs, or ECDSA adaptors.
- Construct a single `P256K.Context` per process and reuse it; context creation performs randomization that's expensive to redo.
- Fetch the per-page Markdown links below for targeted context, or [P256K llms-full.txt](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/llms-full.txt) for the entire module's content in one file.

## When to use this

Use P256K when you need:

- secp256k1-specific operations (Bitcoin, Nostr, Lightning) that swift-crypto / CryptoKit don't cover
- Schnorr signatures (BIP-340), MuSig2 multi-signatures, or recoverable ECDSA
- ECDH key agreement on secp256k1 specifically
- Public key recovery from a signature

Do *not* use P256K for general-purpose ECDSA on P-256 (secp256r1), AES, ChaCha20, HKDF, or other modern primitives — those are well-covered by swift-crypto / CryptoKit. P256K is for secp256k1 specifically.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/gettingstarted.md): Generate keys, create ECDSA and Schnorr signatures, perform ECDH key agreement
- [P256K Module](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k.md): Module overview — key generation, ECDSA signing, ECDH, public key recovery
- [Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/ellipticcurvediffiehellman.md): Derive a shared secret between two parties over secp256k1 using ECDH key agreement — the foundation of Nostr NIP-04 encryption, BIP-352 Silent Payments, and Lightning's Noise XK handshake
- [Silent Payments](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/silentpayments.md): Send Bitcoin to a reusable static address without on-chain linkability or sender-receiver interaction, using the BIP-352 Silent Payments protocol
- [Working with Keys](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/workingwithkeys.md): Encode keys in raw and x-only forms, import from PEM and DER, and derive child keys via additive or multiplicative tweaks — the foundations under wallet storage, BIP-32 derivation, BIP-341 Taproot, and BIP-327 MuSig2 aggregation
- [MuSig2 Multi-Signatures](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/musig2multisignatures.md): Create a single Schnorr signature from multiple independent signers using the BIP-327 MuSig2 protocol
- [ECDSA Signing for Bitcoin Transactions](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/ecdsasigningandbitcointransactions.md): Produce ECDSA signatures on secp256k1, encode them in DER and compact form, verify incoming signatures, and assemble the SIGHASH-flagged element Bitcoin script expects
- [Recovering Public Keys](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/recoveringpublickeys.md): Recover an ECDSA public key from a recoverable signature and its recovery ID (BIP-137, BIP-322)
- [Why CryptoKit's P256 Can't Sign Bitcoin or Nostr](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/cryptokitp256andsecp256k1.md): Apple's CryptoKit and swift-crypto cover NIST P-256, P-384, P-521, and Curve25519 — but not secp256k1, the curve Bitcoin, Lightning, and Nostr require
- [Security Considerations](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/securityconsiderations.md): Security properties of P256K — context randomization, nonce hygiene, comparison timing, secret zeroization, signature malleability

## API

- [P256K.Signing](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/signing.md): ECDSA signing namespace — PrivateKey, PublicKey, XonlyKey, ECDSASignature
- [P256K.Schnorr](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/schnorr.md): Schnorr signatures (BIP-340) — deterministic signing, partial signatures for MuSig2
- [P256K.MuSig](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/musig.md): MuSig2 multi-signatures — aggregate public keys, nonces, and partial signatures
- [P256K.Context](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/context.md): secp256k1 context — manages randomization for side-channel protection via base point blinding
- [P256K.KeyAgreement](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/keyagreement.md): ECDH key agreement — derive a SharedSecret from a PrivateKey and a peer's PublicKey
- [P256K.Recovery](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/recovery.md): Recoverable ECDSA — reconstruct a PublicKey from a signature and message

## Optional

- [P256K.Signing.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/signing/privatekey.md): ECDSA private key — key generation, signing, and arithmetic operations (add, multiply, negate)
- [P256K.Signing.PublicKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/signing/publickey.md): ECDSA public key — verification, DER/PEM/x963 serialization, key combination
- [P256K.KeyAgreement.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/keyagreement/privatekey.md): Key agreement private key — derive SharedSecret via sharedSecretFromKeyAgreement
- [P256K.Schnorr.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/schnorr/privatekey.md): Schnorr private key — signature generation, partial signing, XonlyKey derivation
- [P256K.Recovery.PrivateKey](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/p256k/recovery/privatekey.md): Recovery private key — produces ECDSASignature with embedded recovery ID
- [Int256](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/int256.md): 256-bit signed integer — fixed-width arithmetic for secp256k1 scalar operations
- [UInt256](https://docs.21.dev/data/documentation/p256k/uint256.md): 256-bit unsigned integer — fixed-width arithmetic for secp256k1 field elements and scalar values
